title: Design Process Phases
summary: The design phase is broken into conceptual design, schematic design, and design development subphases.
evidence_quote: The design phase is often broken into conceptual design, schematic design, and design development subphases.

title: Owner's Project Requirements (OPR)
summary: Design should start with a clear statement of design intent, forming the Owner's Project Requirements (OPR) document.
evidence_quote: Design should start with a clear statement of design intent. In commissioning terms, the collective project intents form the Owner’s Project Requirements (OPR) document.

title: Design Intent and Criterion
summary: Each design intent must be paired with a design criterion providing a benchmark for minimum acceptable performance.
evidence_quote: Each design intent must be paired with a design criterion, which provides a benchmark for minimum acceptable performance relative to the intent.

title: Design Validation
summary: Design validation involves estimates, calculations, and simulations to confirm a design option meets criteria.
evidence_quote: Design validation involves the use of a wide range of estimates, calculations, simulations, and related techniques to confirm that a chosen design option will in fact meet the appropriate design criteria.

title: Design vs. Analysis
summary: Design problems have multiple acceptable answers; analysis has a unique solution.
evidence_quote: Design problems are inherently different—much different. ... there are any number of potentially acceptable answers.

title: Iterative Design Process
summary: Design is an iterative process with continuous back-and-forth as the designer selects from available options.
evidence_quote: Design, however, is an iterative process.

title: Predesign Phase Building Program
summary: The building program includes client objectives, space functions, occupant patterns, equipment loads, site location, codes, budget, schedule, and quality.
evidence_quote: The building program should include, but need not be limited to, the following: ...

title: HVAC&R System Program Content
summary: HVAC&R system program includes design temperatures, degree-days, wind velocity, codes, and utility characteristics.
evidence_quote: The information that should be contained in the HVAC&R system program includes design outdoor dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures ...

title: Environmental Conditions Definition
summary: Environmental conditions to be maintained include dry-bulb/wet-bulb temperatures, ventilation, special loads, and acceptable ranges.
evidence_quote: The environmental conditions to be maintained for each building space should be defined by dry-bulb and wet-bulb temperatures ...

title: Schematic Design Evaluation Criteria
summary: Evaluate space requirements, energy use, costs, acoustics, compatibility, IAQ, and aesthetics for each system.
evidence_quote: For each system considered during this phase, evaluate the relative space (and volume) requirements for equipment, ducts, and piping; fuel and/or electrical use ...

title: Coordination with Other Disciplines
summary: Schematic HVAC design coordinates with architectural, structural, electrical, and fire protection systems.
evidence_quote: The schematic HVAC design is used to coordinate critical HVAC&R system requirements with the architectural, structural, electrical, and fire protection systems ...

title: Energy Code Compliance
summary: State or local building or energy codes must be considered, including restrictions on glass, envelope, lighting, and HVAC systems.
evidence_quote: The requirements of state or local building or energy codes ... must also be considered at this point because of restrictions on the amount of glass, other envelope assembly requirements, lighting, HVAC&R system and equipment limitations ...

title: Design Development Calculations
summary: Heat loss, heat gain, and ventilation calculations are refined to design air and water distribution systems and select equipment.
evidence_quote: Heat loss, heat gain, and ventilation calculations are refined and used to design the air and water distribution systems and to select equipment.

title: Owner Approval of Preliminary Design
summary: After preliminary drawings, outline specifications, and cost estimates are approved by the owner, the project scope is finalized.
evidence_quote: After preliminary drawings, outline specifications, and cost estimates have been approved by the owner, the project scope and solution are essentially finalized.
